title: No tri-state output when assigning 1'bz
body:
I have the following .v file:

```
module tritest(
        output my_tri_out,
        inout my_tri_inout,

        input my_in0,
        input my_in1
        );

        assign my_tri_out = 1'bz;

        assign my_tri_inout = my_in0 ? my_in1 : 1'bz;

endmodule
```

This generates the following .blif:
```
.model tritest
.inputs my_in0 my_in1
.outputs my_tri_out my_tri_inout
.names $false
.names $true
1
.names $undef
.gate $_TBUF_ A=my_in1 E=my_in0 Y=my_tri_inout
.attr src "tristate.v:12"
.names $undef my_tri_out
1 1
.end
```

When running this through arachne, I get this:
```
# arachne-pnr 0.1+284+0 (git sha1 05ea734, g++ 5.4.0-6ubuntu1~16.04.9 -O2)
.model tritest
.inputs my_in0 my_in1
.outputs my_tri_out my_tri_inout
.names $undef
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_in0 LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE= D_OUT_0= D_OUT_1= D_IN_0=my_in0$2 D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 000001
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_in1 LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE= D_OUT_0= D_OUT_1= D_IN_0=my_in1$2 D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 000001
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_tri_inout LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE=my_in0$2 D_OUT_0=my_in1$2 D_OUT_1= D_IN_0= D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 101001
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_tri_out LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE= D_OUT_0=$undef D_OUT_1= D_IN_0= D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 011001
.end
```

This issue is with the ```my_tri_out``` pin: instead of having an SB_IO with PIN_TYPE 101001 with OUTPUT_ENABLE  strapped to 0, it has an output-always-active SB_IO with PIN_TYPE 011001.

Yosys is very clear about limited support for tri-state, but the Reddit comment [here](https://www.reddit.com/r/yosys/comments/60yqrq/limited_support_for_tristate_logic/) made me think that things would be fine for a full iceStorm flow, and projects like BlackIce-II have these kind of unsupported 1'bz assignments in their examples everywhere. 

The minimal purpose of filing this issue is to have it documented so that it shows up for others when they google about it, but the better solution would be for Yosys to output something like this instead:
```
.gate $_TBUF_ A=$undef E=$false Y=my_tri_out
```

Tom
code_blocks:
.model tritest
.inputs my_in0 my_in1
.outputs my_tri_out my_tri_inout
.names $false
.names $true
1
.names $undef
.gate $_TBUF_ A=my_in1 E=my_in0 Y=my_tri_inout
.attr src "tristate.v:12"
.names $undef my_tri_out
1 1
.end

# arachne-pnr 0.1+284+0 (git sha1 05ea734, g++ 5.4.0-6ubuntu1~16.04.9 -O2)
.model tritest
.inputs my_in0 my_in1
.outputs my_tri_out my_tri_inout
.names $undef
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_in0 LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE= D_OUT_0= D_OUT_1= D_IN_0=my_in0$2 D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 000001
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_in1 LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE= D_OUT_0= D_OUT_1= D_IN_0=my_in1$2 D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 000001
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_tri_inout LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE=my_in0$2 D_OUT_0=my_in1$2 D_OUT_1= D_IN_0= D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 101001
.gate SB_IO PACKAGE_PIN=my_tri_out LATCH_INPUT_VALUE= CLOCK_ENABLE= INPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_CLK= OUTPUT_ENABLE= D_OUT_0=$undef D_OUT_1= D_IN_0= D_IN_1=
.param PIN_TYPE 011001
.end
